cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we know about garage sales based on the provided passage. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the text and read the part about garage sales. The passage says: "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture. These are held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good." So it mentions that garage sales are part of American culture and are held in spring and fall. Option A says it's only held in spring. But the text says "usually in the spring and fall", so it's not only spring. A is incorrect. Option B: "The goods on display are totally useless." The passage says that the homeowner is clearing out items that are no longer useful to him. But the text also mentions that the goods are in good condition and great buys can be found. So they're not totally useless. B is incorrect. Option C: "Garage sales are popular with Americans." The text states that garage sales have become "very much a part of American culture." That definitely suggests they are popular. C is likely correct. Option D: "All the goods are selling far below their real value."
